prompt:
Scheduler infra. We run two copies of the ingest scheduler and today they "coordinate" via a Redis SETNX hack that split-brained us twice last quarter. I want proper Kubernetes leader leases. The repo already has k8s_client.py — a small working ApiClient (bearer auth, JSON, get/create/replace lease calls, Status-body errors as ApiError, and it deliberately refuses redirects). That part is done and its behavior must not change. What's missing is the actual lease logic.

Before touching code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to confirm the CURRENT official Kubernetes docs for the coordination.k8s.io/v1 Lease API — the Lease reference page (spec fields, which of them are MicroTime, the read/replace operations) and the API concepts page for how resourceVersion optimistic concurrency and 409 Conflict recovery actually work. Our pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json, provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both protected. If the live docs contradict the fixtures, say so explicitly in your summary and leave fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final message.

The feature — new module lease_renewer.py:

- format_micro_time(dt) — serialize an aware UTC datetime the way the Lease API's MicroTime fields go over the wire. Look at the current docs for what MicroTime means; the exact canonical form we pin is in docs/contract.json, and yes, the padding matters.
- class LeadershipLost(Exception).
- class LeaseRenewer(client, namespace, name, identity, lease_duration_seconds=15, clock=None) — clock is a callable returning an aware UTC datetime; NEVER call datetime.now yourself when one is injected, the tests drive time. Exposes .is_leader (bool).
- .acquire() -> bool: read the lease. Missing -> create it (full body — the group/version/kind and which spec fields a fresh lease carries are things you should be able to defend from the docs). Held by someone else and still fresh -> back off, return False, and don't you dare write anything. Expired (the lease's own duration counts, measured from its renewTime) -> take it over with a replace that echoes the resourceVersion you just read — transitions, acquireTime, renewTime semantics per docs/contract.json. If the takeover 409s, someone beat us; cede, no retry. Re-acquiring a lease we already hold is a renewal, not a transition.
- .renew(): fresh GET first. If the holder isn't us anymore -> raise LeadershipLost and do not PUT. Otherwise replace with renewTime advanced to now, preserving acquireTime and leaseTransitions, echoing the fresh resourceVersion. On 409: exactly one retry through another fresh GET (rival holder -> LeadershipLost; second 409 -> let the ApiError propagate).

test_lease_renewer.py is the acceptance contract — it runs a loopback fake API server (dummy token, no cluster) and also re-checks the existing client behavior, which must keep passing. Run python3 test_lease_renewer.py until green. The test file and everything under docs/ are protected.

research.interface: coordination.k8s.io/v1 Lease read/replace with resourceVersion optimistic concurrency; researched against v1.36

official_sources.title: Lease | Kubernetes (kubernetes-api reference)
official_sources.used_for: LeaseSpec field set (holderIdentity, leaseDurationSeconds, acquireTime, renewTime, leaseTransitions), the MicroTime typing of acquireTime/renewTime, and the GET/POST/PUT operation paths under /apis/coordination.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/leases

official_sources.title: Kubernetes API Concepts
official_sources.used_for: metadata.resourceVersion optimistic-concurrency semantics for updates (409 Conflict on stale versions and re-read recovery) and the meta/v1 Status error envelope

official_sources.title: Kubernetes v1.36 official OpenAPI specification (kubernetes/kubernetes, release-1.36)
official_sources.used_for: the exact LeaseSpec property descriptions, the MicroTime definition ('version of Time with microsecond level precision', string/date-time), the PUT replace operation's 200/201/401 responses, and Status fields

research.language_rationale: The official kubernetes Python client exists, but its leader-election helper is a wrapper over exactly this read/replace-with-resourceVersion REST loop, and the harness forbids third-party packages; the Lease API is plain JSON REST, so stdlib urllib in a small scheduler sidecar is a credible, idiomatic integration surface.
